tasks.title: Add X key to close any dialog + create missing dialogs
tasks.description: Create intro dialog and throne dialog that don't exist yet, then add a universal 'X' keypress handler that dismisses whichever dialog is currently open. Update the hotkey modal table to document the new shortcut.
tasks.acceptance_criteria: 1. Pressing X closes whichever dialog is open (intro, call-earth, throne, hotkey modal). 2. Intro dialog shown on game start; X triggers Begin. 3. Call earth dialog closed by X. 4. Throne dialog shown on victory; X triggers Continue. 5. Hotkey modal closed by X. 6. X does nothing when no dialog is open. 7. Hotkey table lists X as 'Close dialog'. 8. Existing close mechanisms still work.
tasks.claudeMd: # Project Context You are implementing a universal 'X' key dismiss for dialogs in a vanilla JS browser game. The project is a single-page app: `index.html`, `style.css`, `game.js` (single IIFE, ~1730 lines). ## Existing Dialog Patterns ### Call Earth Dialog (`#call-earth-dialog`) - State: `state.callEarthDialogOpen` (boolean) - Open: `dialogEl.classList.remove('hidden')` + set state true (line 1159-1160) - Close: `closeCallEarthDialog()` — sets state false, adds 'hidden' class (line 1164-1167) - HTML: `index.html` lines 63-73, uses `.modal-backdrop` + `.modal-content` + `.modal-close` classes ### Hotkey Modal (`#hotkey-modal`) - State: `state.hotkeyModalOpen` (boolean) - Toggle: `toggleHotkeyModal()` — flips state, toggles 'hidden' class (line 1538-1541) - HTML: `index.html` lines 74-103 ## Dialogs You Must Create ### Intro Dialog - Show on game load (before gameplay starts). Must have a 'Begin' button. - Add `state.introDialogOpen: true` to initial state (line 67-87). - Init it as visible (no 'hidden' class). Clicking Begin or pressing X calls a `dismissIntroDialog()` function that hides it and sets `state.introDialogOpen = false`. - Block all game input while intro is showing — add `if (state.introDialogOpen) return;` in the keydown handler alongside the existing modal/dialog checks (after the X key handler). - Place HTML before `#call-earth-dialog` in `index.html`. - Content: Game title 'Elon's Bizarre Adventure', brief flavor text about crash-landing on Mars, and a 'Begin' button. ### Throne Dialog (Victory) - Show when Mars Throne is built (in `buildMarsThrone()`, line 1235). - Add `state.throneDialogOpen: false` to state. - After building the throne, set `state.throneDialogOpen = true` and show the dialog. - 'Continue' button or pressing X calls a function that hides the dialog and sets `state.throneDialogOpen = false`. The game is already over (`state.gameOver = true`) so no further action needed. - Place HTML after `#call-earth-dialog` in `index.html`. - Content: Victory message about claiming Mars. ## Universal X Key Handler In the `keydown` listener (line 1623), add X key handling BEFORE the ESC handler: ```js if (e.key === 'x' || e.key === 'X') { if (state.introDialogOpen) { dismissIntroDialog(); e.preventDefault(); return; } if (state.callEarthDialogOpen) { closeCallEarthDialog(); e.preventDefault(); return; } if (state.throneDialogOpen) { dismissThroneDialog(); e.preventDefault(); return; } if (state.hotkeyModalOpen) { toggleHotkeyModal(); e.preventDefault(); return; } // No dialog open — do nothing, fall through to default return return; } ``` IMPORTANT: The X handler must come before the line `if (state.hotkeyModalOpen) return;` (line 1632) so it can close the hotkey modal. It must also come before `if (state.callEarthDialogOpen) return;` (line 1633) so it can close that dialog. ## Hotkey Table Update In `index.html`, add a row to the hotkey table (before the ESC row): ```html XClose dialog ``` ## CSS Use the same `.modal-backdrop`, `.modal-content`, `.modal-header`, `.modal-close` patterns. Add IDs `#intro-dialog` and `#throne-dialog` with the same positioning as `#call-earth-dialog` and `#hotkey-modal`. Copy the existing hidden/visible toggle pattern. ## Conventions - Vanilla JS, no modules, everything inside the IIFE - State managed on the `state` object - DOM toggling uses `.hidden` class - Use `var` for consistency with existing code (some newer code uses `const`/`let` — follow the local pattern of whichever section you're editing) - Add click listeners in the same section as existing ones (around line 1615) ## Gotchas - The 'x' key is NOT currently used for any game action, so no conflict. - Must handle both 'x' and 'X' (case-insensitive). - The intro dialog must block the init canvas rendering — the game should still initialize behind it, but input should be blocked. - Don't break ESC handling for hotkey modal — ESC should still work. - The throne dialog shows AFTER `state.gameOver = true`, so game input is already blocked. The X handler just needs to dismiss the dialog overlay.

checklist.description: Create intro dialog HTML/CSS that shows on game load with a Begin button
steps: Add #intro-dialog markup to index.html before #call-earth-dialog, using .modal-backdrop + .modal-content pattern
Add CSS for #intro-dialog and #intro-dialog.hidden in style.css
Add state.introDialogOpen = true to state object in game.js
Verify dialog is visible on page load (no .hidden class initially)

checklist.description: Implement dismissIntroDialog() that hides intro and allows gameplay
steps: Create dismissIntroDialog() function in game.js that sets state.introDialogOpen = false and adds .hidden class
Wire Begin button click to dismissIntroDialog()
Add state.introDialogOpen check to keydown handler to block input while intro is showing
Verify clicking Begin hides the dialog and enables gameplay

checklist.description: Create throne dialog HTML/CSS shown on Mars Throne victory
steps: Add #throne-dialog markup to index.html after #call-earth-dialog with Continue button
Add CSS for #throne-dialog and #throne-dialog.hidden in style.css
Add state.throneDialogOpen = false to state object
Modify buildMarsThrone() to set state.throneDialogOpen = true and show the dialog
Create dismissThroneDialog() that hides the dialog and sets state to false
Wire Continue button click to dismissThroneDialog()

checklist.description: Add universal X key handler in keydown listener
steps: Add X key check at the top of the keydown handler (before ESC and before modal/dialog blocking checks)
Handle case-insensitive X (both 'x' and 'X')
For intro dialog: call dismissIntroDialog()
For call-earth dialog: call closeCallEarthDialog()
For throne dialog: call dismissThroneDialog()
For hotkey modal: call toggleHotkeyModal()
If no dialog open, do nothing (return without interfering with gameplay)

checklist.description: Update hotkey modal table to list X as Close dialog
steps: Add XClose dialog to hotkey table in index.html before the ESC row
Verify the new row appears in the hotkey modal

checklist.description: Existing close mechanisms still work (ESC for hotkey modal, x button, action buttons)
steps: Verify ESC still toggles the hotkey modal
Verify the x (times) close buttons on call-earth and hotkey dialogs still work
Verify Begin button on intro dialog still works
Verify Continue button on throne dialog still works

quality_checklist.description: X key handler follows priority order and doesn't interfere with gameplay when no dialog is open
steps: Check that the X handler is placed before the modal/dialog blocking checks in the keydown listener
Check that 'x' is not accidentally mapped to any game action
Check that pressing X with no dialog open doesn't trigger any side effects

quality_checklist.description: New dialogs follow existing codebase patterns consistently
steps: Check that intro-dialog and throne-dialog use the same CSS classes as existing modals
Check that state flags follow the same naming pattern (camelCase, boolean)
Check that open/close functions follow the same pattern as closeCallEarthDialog() and toggleHotkeyModal()

quality_checklist.description: No dead code, unused variables, or redundant checks introduced
steps: Check for any unused functions or variables
Check for duplicate state checks in the keydown handler
Verify no CSS rules are duplicated unnecessarily

checks.description: Intro dialog shows on load and X key dismisses it
steps: Open http://localhost:3000
Verify intro dialog is visible with game title and Begin button
Press X key
Verify intro dialog is dismissed and game is playable

checks.description: Hotkey modal can be opened with ESC and closed with X
steps: Dismiss intro dialog first
Press ESC to open hotkey modal
Verify X key row is listed in the hotkey table
Press X to close the hotkey modal
Verify modal is closed

checks.description: X key does nothing when no dialog is open
steps: With no dialog open, press X key
Verify no dialog appears and gameplay is unaffected

checks.description: Existing ESC and button close mechanisms still work
steps: Press ESC to open hotkey modal, press ESC again to close — verify it works
Open hotkey modal, click the x button — verify it closes
Reload page, click Begin button — verify intro dialog closes
